consistency_check.notes: The feature is standard for the Ranger class at level 8.

quality_assessment.clarity: High. The text clearly defines movement benefits and hazard negation.
quality_assessment.completeness: Complete. It covers nonmagical difficult terrain, nonmagical plants (including hazards), and magical plant impediments.

effect_1.name: Ignore Nonmagical Difficult Terrain Cost
effect_1.rule_comparison: Standard D&D 5e implementation of Land's Stride.

effect_2.name: Plant Passage
effect_2.mechanic: Ignore slowing effects and damage from nonmagical plants with hazards (thorns, spines).

effect_3.name: Magical Plant Resistance
effect_3.mechanic: Advantage on saving throws against spells/effects like *entangle* that manipulate plants to impede movement.

mechanics_review.overall_power_level: Appropriate for a Level 8 Tier 2 feature. Excellent utility for wilderness traversal.

analysis.archivist_notes: The data entry is accurate and matches established 5e rules for the Ranger's Land's Stride feature. No mechanical ambiguities or errors detected.
